steps.title: Interpret the percentage
steps.content:
When a substance can contain a maximum of 0.001% phosphorus by mass, the mass of phosphorus is 0.001% of the total sample mass:

$$ \text{mass of P} = 0.001\% \times 140 \text{ g} $$

steps.title: Convert percentage to decimal
steps.content:
To compute with the percentage, convert it to decimal form by dividing by 100:

$$ 0.001\% = \frac{0.001}{100} = 0.00001 $$

steps.title: Calculate the maximum phosphorus mass
steps.content:
Now multiply the decimal form by the sample mass:

$$ \text{mass of P} = 0.00001 \times 140 = 0.0014 \text{ grams} $$

The maximum mass of phosphorus the sample can contain is 0.0014 grams.
